sentence_reviews.text: Content provenance records origin, transformations, model or tool identity, and integrity metadata through manifests, attestations, or verifiable logs.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: C2PA specification defines content credentials/manifests with provenance, assertions, ingredients/actions, signing, and validation metadata.
sentence_reviews.notes: Directly supported for manifests, provenance, transformations/actions, identity metadata, and integrity/signing; verifiable logs is broader but consistent.

sentence_reviews.text: It supports auditable lineage even when statistical AI detection is unreliable or content passes through multiple tools.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: C2PA is designed to convey provenance and content credentials across asset creation/editing workflows via signed manifests and ingredients.
sentence_reviews.notes: Auditable lineage and multi-tool provenance are supported; the contrast with statistical AI detection is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: C2PA manifests can be signed and can include assertions about generation or creation actions.
C2PA supports ingredient and action assertions that describe asset derivation and edits.
C2PA focuses on content asset provenance; dataset lineage is analogous but not specifically established in the packet source.
sentence_reviews.notes: Plausible extension, but not directly supported by C2PA content credential specification alone.

sentence_reviews.text: Metadata can be stripped, adoption is uneven, and provenance establishes origin rather than truth or safety.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: C2PA provenance relies on attached/associated manifests and trust validation; it establishes provenance/integrity metadata rather than semantic truth.
sentence_reviews.notes: Origin-not-truth and metadata dependence are supported; uneven adoption is plausible but not directly sourced.

sentence_reviews.text: Content credentials, signing & provenance measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Provenance, citations & evidence tracking.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: C2PA supports provenance tracking, assertions, ingredients, signing, and verification of content metadata.

sentence_reviews.text: Content credentials, signing & provenance measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Privacy, governance, licensing & audit.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: C2PA supports credentials and provenance governance; NIST supports privacy/governance and auditability concerns.

sentence_reviews.text: Content credentials, signing & provenance measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Information theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: C2PA uses signatures/integrity metadata, but the packet source does not directly establish information theory as a dependency.
sentence_reviews.notes: Cryptographic/integrity concepts are relevant, but information theory is indirect.

edge_reviews.notes: Provenance/evidence tracking is core to content credentials.
Governance and audit concerns are core for content provenance deployment.
Integrity/signing are supported, but information theory is an indirect dependency and common strength is not clearly established.
